title: polycardia aquifolium var. aquifolium
intro: Polycardia aquifolium var. aquifolium, also known as European Holly, is a plant species in the Aquifoliaceae family, characterized by its glossy leaves and red berries. Traditionally, European Holly has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote healing in various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy for colds, coughs, and other infections. European Holly can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to harness its health benefits.
